rationale: Both agree with the actual 'duplicate of #4808' closure, but X's own findings note PR #4808 is CLOSED (not merged) yet it still confidently declares the bug 'already fixed on main' — an unresolved internal contradiction that risks misleading the reporter about whether the fix actually landed upstream. Y catches exactly this gap, explicitly flagging that #4808 never merged and asking for confirmation, which is the correctly-hedged move on a genuinely ambiguous point. Y is also more grounded (traces the 5 call-sites from #4809, cites specific PR merge states and test file line ranges, honestly scopes its verification to the local dev branch) and more complete (covers the tracking history plus a concrete regression-test coverage hole), whereas X's answer is narrower and internally inconsistent.
